title: How Can I Use monday.com to Manage Real-Time Gold Price Updates?

posts.author: Jamzee (JAME)
posts.cookedHtml:
I run a website tracking gold prices in Turkey and I’m looking to integrate a live update workflow using monday.com. I’d love to know:
What’s the best way to automatically import live gold price data into monday.com?
Should I use “monday.com Apps & Integrations” or build custom workflows with the API?
How have other users managed “real-time external feeds” using monday.com boards or dashboards?

I’d appreciate any advice or examples of similar setups you’ve done, thanks in advance!

posts.author: AshcroftJewellery (Christian Fox )
posts.cookedHtml:
I tried to do this with MondayVibe, but for some reason, it would never get the price of gold accurate.

Gold, as of writing this comment is currently priced at: £3,039.86

Monday Vibe shows the gold price to be: £1,935.86

As you can see, this was completely unreliable for us. We bloody wish gold was that price!!

posts.author: anowacka (Anna from SolDevelo)
posts.cookedHtml:
What source do you use to get the gold prices? Does it have some API available? If so, setting up even a simple workflow with make.com can do its’ work. If not, I would try to use a scraper or maybe build some AI agent, but it may give you false prices unfortunately. Relying on API should give you the best and most accurate results.

posts.author: willsamadi (Will Samadi)
posts.cookedHtml: I would say the easiest way is to use a third-party automation like n8n or make. Let me know if you want me to dig into the details.
